title: Add NEURGenerator for ESP32-S3 to build
author: pollinations-ai[bot]

gist.headline: Pollinations on a microchip
gist.blurb: The NEURGenerator brings Pollinations to the ESP32-S3 microcontroller. Proof that you don't need a massive server rack to make AI magic happen—just a tiny chip and some good vibes.
gist.summary: Added the NEURGenerator project to the community showcase, demonstrating Pollinations API integration on an ESP32-S3 microcontroller with PSRAM support.
gist.impact: Hardware hackers and IoT tinkerers now have a reference implementation for bringing AI generation to physical devices.
gist.visual_concept: Hardware tinkering and microcontrollers. Polly the bee acting as an electrical engineer, working on a tiny glowing microchip (ESP32). The robot companion assisting.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny safety goggles, soldering wires onto a glowing microcontroller chip. A chunky pixel robot with a CRT monitor head watches excitedly, holding a tiny blueprint. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
